content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


August 30, 2002


Charles R. Green, City Attorney
Green, de Bortnowsky & Quintanilla, LLP
23801 Calabasas Road, Suite 1015
Calabasas, CA 91302

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-208

Dear Mr. Green:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

After a careful review of the facts contained in your request, we decline to provide written advice to you. Our advice would involve analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Moreover, given the totality of the circumstances surrounding your request and the nature of the decisions at issue, our written advice would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

KLG:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\02-208

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

Our advice would involve analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(bX8XA).) Moreover, given the totality of the circumstances surrounding your request and the nature of the decisions at issue, our written advice would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest (Regulation 1 8329(bX8XF).)

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to issue an advice letter to City Attorney Charles R. Green regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, citing Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and (F) as the basis for declining to provide written advice on matters involving past conduct and decisions.
